name.description: Immutable. The name of the custom constraint. This is unique within the organization.

condition.description: A CEL condition that refers to a supported service resource, for example `resource.management.autoUpgrade == false`. For details about CEL usage, see [Common Expression Language](https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/creating-managing-custom-constraints#common_expression_language).

action_type.description: The action to take if the condition is met. Possible values are: `ALLOW`, `DENY`.

method_types.description: A list of RESTful methods for which to enforce the constraint. Can be `CREATE`, `UPDATE`, or both. Not all Google Cloud services support both methods. To see supported methods for each service, find the service in [Supported services](https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/custom-constraint-supported-services).

resource_types.description: Immutable. The fully qualified name of the Google Cloud REST resource containing the object and field you want to restrict. For example, `container.googleapis.com/NodePool`.

parent.description: The parent of the resource, an organization. Format should be `organizations/{organization_id}`.

display_name.description: A human-friendly name for the constraint.

description.description: A human-friendly description of the constraint to display as an error message when the policy is violated.
